revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神|名=賀茂建角身命|神祇=[[天津神]]|全名=賀茂建角身命|別名=鴨建角身命、[[八咫烏]]、八咫烏鴨武角身命、三嶋湟咋、三島溝咋、三島溝橛耳神、陶津耳命、陶津耳、天日方奇日方武茅淳祇|神階=|神格=|陵所=|父=[[天櫛玉命]]|母=|親=|配偶者=[[伊加古夜比売]]|子=[[玉依彦命]]
[[活玉依毘売]]（玉依姫）|宮=|神社=[[賀茂御祖神社]] など|記紀=|関連氏族=[[賀茂氏]]（鴨県主）、[[葛城国造]]、[[三輪氏]]}}'''賀茂建角身命'''（かもたけつぬみのみこと、かもたけつのみのみこと）は、[[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]に登場する[[Kami|神]]である。'''鴨建角身命'''とも呼ばれるブリタニカ国際大百科事典-小項目電子辞書版。 。 == 概要 == 別名には'''八咫烏'''、'''八咫烏鴨武角身命'''（やたからすかもたけつのみのみこと）、'''三嶋湟咋'''（みしまのみぞくい）、'''三島溝咋'''、'''三島溝橛耳神'''（みしまのみぞくいみみのかみ）、'''陶津耳命'''（すえつみみのみこと）、'''陶津耳'''、'''天日方奇日方武茅淳祇'''（あまひがたくしひがたたけちぬつみ）がある。 [[Yamashiro Province|山城]]の[[Kamo clan|賀茂氏（賀茂県主）]]や葛城国造の始祖であり、[[Shimogamo Shrine|賀茂御祖神社]]（下鴨神社）の祭神として知られる。 『[[Shinsen Shōjiroku|新撰姓氏録]]』によれば、賀茂建角身命は[[Kamimusubi|神魂命]]（かみむすびのみこと）の孫である。神武東征の際、[[Takamimusubi|高木神]]・[[Amaterasu|天照大神]]の命を受けて日向の曾の峰に天降り、[[Yamato Province|大和]]の葛木山に至り、八咫烏に化身して[[Emperor Jimmu|神武天皇]]を先導し、金鵄として勝利に貢献した。 『山城国風土記』（逸文）によれば、大和の葛木山から山代の岡田の賀茂（岡田鴨神社がある）に至り、葛野河（高野川）と賀茂河（[[Kamo River|鴨川]]）が合流する地点（下鴨神社がある）に鎮まった。 賀茂建角身命には建玉依比古命（たけたまよりひこのみこと）と建玉依比売命（建玉依姫命、たけたまよりひめのみこと）の2柱の御子神がいる。建玉依比古命は後に賀茂県主となる。建玉依比売命は、丹塗矢に化身した[[Yakusanoikazuchi|火雷神]]（ほのいかづちのかみ）を床の近くに置いていたところ、賀茂別雷命（かもわけいかづちのみこと、[[Kamigamo Shrine|上賀茂神社]]の祭神）を懐妊し出産したとされるが（『山城国風土記』逸文）、『[[Kojiki|古事記]]』や『[[Nihon Shoki|日本書紀]]』、『[[Kujiki|先代旧事本紀]]』では[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]または[[Ōmononushi|大物主神]]の妻となり、櫛御方命、[[Himetataraisuzu-hime|媛蹈鞴五十鈴媛命]]、[[Isuzuyori-hime|五十鈴依姫命]]を生んだとされる。 == 系譜 == 賀茂氏の系譜は「[[Kamimusubi|神皇産霊尊]]ー天神玉命ー'''[[Futodama|天櫛玉命]]'''ー加茂建角身命（八咫烏鴨武角身命）ー'''鴨建玉依彦命'''ー五十手美命（賀茂氏祖）」とされ、陶津耳命の系譜は「[[Takamimusubi|高皇産霊尊]]ー'''天活玉命'''ー天押立命ー陶津耳命ー'''玉依彦命'''ー生玉兄日子命（賀茂氏祖）」となっている。また『難波田使首系図』には「'''天活玉命'''（伊久魂命）ー天押立命（'''神櫛玉命'''）ー陶津耳命ー'''玉依彦命'''」と続いており、これに従えば「天神玉命（天活玉命、伊久魂命）ー天櫛玉命（神櫛玉命、天押立命）ー加茂建角身命（八咫烏、陶津耳命）ー鴨建玉依彦命（玉依彦命）ー五十手美命（生玉兄日子命）」となる[[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族の研究⑬　天皇氏族 天孫族の来た道』青垣出版、2018年。 。 娘には[[Emperor Jimmu|神武天皇]]の妻である[[Himetataraisuzu-hime|比売多々良伊須気余理比売]]の母・勢夜陀多良比売（活玉依毘売、玉櫛媛、玉依姫命、建玉依姫命）がいる。言い換えれば、加茂建角身命は神武天皇の岳母の父にあたり、加茂建角身命を八咫烏と同一神とすると、世代関係が大きく矛盾してしまうことになるため、実際に神武天皇の道案内をしたのは加茂建角身命の孫である生玉兄日子命と考えられている宝賀寿男『古代氏族の研究⑬　天皇氏族 天孫族の来た道』青垣出版、2018年。 。 なお、八咫烏は『[[Kojiki|古事記]]』や『[[Nihon Shoki|日本書紀]]』に登場するが、『日本書紀』では、同じ神武東征の場面で、金鵄（金色の[[Black kite|トビ]]）が長髄彦との戦いで神武天皇を助けたともされており、天日鷲神の別名である天加奈止美命（あめのかなとみ）の名称が金鵄（かなとび）に通じることから、天日鷲神、賀茂建角身命と同一視する説を[[Hirata Atsutane|平田篤胤]]などが唱えている宝賀寿男「神武天皇、実在の可能性」『「神武天皇」伝承の真実を検証する⑦』、2017年。 。 == 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}{{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[Shimogamo Shrine|賀茂御祖神社]] * [[List of Japanese deities|日本の神の一覧]] [[Category:Japanese gods]]

'''Kamo Kenkakumikami no Mikoto''' is a [[Kami|God]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. Kamotaketsunomikoto''', also known as '''Kamotaketsunomikoto''',<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>.
== 概要 == 別名には'''八咫烏'''、'''八咫烏鴨武角身命'''（やたからすかもたけつのみのみこと）、'''三嶋湟咋'''（みしまのみぞくい）、'''三島溝咋'''、'''三島溝橛耳神'''（みしまのみぞくいみみのかみ）、'''陶津耳命'''（すえつみみのみこと）、'''陶津耳'''、'''天日方奇日方武茅淳祇'''（あまひがたくしひがたたけちぬつみ）がある。
[[Yamashiro Province|山城]]の[[Kamo clan|賀茂氏（賀茂県主）]]や葛城国造の始祖であり、[[Shimogamo Shrine|賀茂御祖神社]]（下鴨神社）の祭神として知られる。
『[[Shinsen Shōjiroku|新撰姓氏録]]』によれば、賀茂建角身命は[[Kamimusubi|神魂命]]（かみむすびのみこと）の孫である。神武東征の際、[[Takamimusubi|高木神]]・[[Amaterasu|天照大神]]の命を受けて日向の曾の峰に天降り、[[Yamato Province|大和]]の葛木山に至り、八咫烏に化身して[[Emperor Jimmu|神武天皇]]を先導し、金鵄として勝利に貢献した。
『山城国風土記』（逸文）によれば、大和の葛木山から山代の岡田の賀茂（岡田鴨神社がある）に至り、葛野河（高野川）と賀茂河（[[Kamo River|鴨川]]）が合流する地点（下鴨神社がある）に鎮まった。
賀茂建角身命には建玉依比古命（たけたまよりひこのみこと）と建玉依比売命（建玉依姫命、たけたまよりひめのみこと）の2柱の御子神がいる。建玉依比古命は後に賀茂県主となる。建玉依比売命は、丹塗矢に化身した[[Yakusanoikazuchi|火雷神]]（ほのいかづちのかみ）を床の近くに置いていたところ、賀茂別雷命（かもわけいかづちのみこと、[[Kamigamo Shrine|上賀茂神社]]の祭神）を懐妊し出産したとされるが（『山城国風土記』逸文）、『[[Kojiki|古事記]]』や『[[Nihon Shoki|日本書紀]]』、『[[Kujiki|先代旧事本紀]]』では[[Kotoshironushi|事代主神]]または[[Ōmononushi|大物主神]]の妻となり、櫛御方命、[[Himetataraisuzu-hime|媛蹈鞴五十鈴媛命]]、[[Isuzuyori-hime|五十鈴依姫命]]を生んだとされる。
== 系譜 == 賀茂氏の系譜は「[[Kamimusubi|神皇産霊尊]]ー天神玉命ー'''[[Futodama|天櫛玉命]]'''ー加茂建角身命（八咫烏鴨武角身命）ー'''鴨建玉依彦命'''ー五十手美命（賀茂氏祖）」とされ、陶津耳命の系譜は「[[Takamimusubi|高皇産霊尊]]ー'''天活玉命'''ー天押立命ー陶津耳命ー'''玉依彦命'''ー生玉兄日子命（賀茂氏祖）」となっている。また『難波田使首系図』には「'''天活玉命'''（伊久魂命）ー天押立命（'''神櫛玉命'''）ー陶津耳命ー'''玉依彦命'''」と続いており、これに従えば「天神玉命（天活玉命、伊久魂命）ー天櫛玉命（神櫛玉命、天押立命）ー加茂建角身命（八咫烏、陶津耳命）ー鴨建玉依彦命（玉依彦命）ー五十手美命（生玉兄日子命）」となる<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族の研究⑬ 天皇氏族 天孫族の来た道』青垣出版、2018年。 </ref>。
娘には[[Emperor Jimmu|神武天皇]]の妻である[[Himetataraisuzu-hime|比売多々良伊須気余理比売]]の母・勢夜陀多良比売（活玉依毘売、玉櫛媛、玉依姫命、建玉依姫命）がいる。言い換えれば、加茂建角身命は神武天皇の岳母の父にあたり、加茂建角身命を八咫烏と同一神とすると、世代関係が大きく矛盾してしまうことになるため、実際に神武天皇の道案内をしたのは加茂建角身命の孫である生玉兄日子命と考えられている<ref>宝賀寿男『古代氏族の研究⑬ 天皇氏族 天孫族の来た道』青垣出版、2018年。 </ref>。
なお、八咫烏は『[[Kojiki|古事記]]』や『[[Nihon Shoki|日本書紀]]』に登場するが、『日本書紀』では、同じ神武東征の場面で、金鵄（金色の[[Black kite|トビ]]）が長髄彦との戦いで神武天皇を助けたともされており、天日鷲神の別名である天加奈止美命（あめのかなとみ）の名称が金鵄（かなとび）に通じることから、天日鷲神、賀茂建角身命と同一視する説を[[Hirata Atsutane|平田篤胤]]などが唱えている<ref>宝賀寿男「神武天皇、実在の可能性」『「神武天皇」伝承の真実を検証する⑦』、2017年。 </ref>。
== 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}{{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 ==
* [[Shimogamo Shrine|賀茂御祖神社]] * [[List of Japanese deities|日本の神の一覧]] [[Category:Japanese gods]]

'''Kamo Kenkakumikami no Mikoto''' is a [[Kami|God]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. Kamotaketsunomikoto''', also known as '''Kamotaketsunomikoto''',<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>.
== Overview == He is also known by the names '''Yatagarasu''', '''Yatagarasu Kamo Takekazumi no Mikoto''', '''Mishima no Mizokui''', '''Mishima Mizokui''', '''Mishima Mizokusaikomi no Kami'', '''Suetsumimi no Mikoto''', '''Suetsumimi''' and '''Amahigatakushihigata Takechinutsumi'''.
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[Yamashiro Province]], the [[Kamo Prefecture]] and the Katsuragi Province, and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsengumi Roku]], Kamo Kenkakumimikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi|Kamitonomikoto]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto]], under the command of [[Takamimusubi|Takagi-kami]] and [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamo Kenkakumimikoto had two sons, Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto and Taketamayorihime no-mikoto. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[[Himetataraisuzu-haru Himetataraisuzu-hime|Hime Totobori Isuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyori-hime]].
== 系譜 == 賀茂氏の系譜は「[[Kamimusubi|神皇産霊尊]]ー天神玉命ー'''[[Futodama|天櫛玉命]]'''ー加茂建角身命（八咫烏鴨武角身命）ー'''鴨建玉依彦命'''ー五十手美命（賀茂氏祖）」とされ、陶津耳命の系譜は「[[Takamimusubi|高皇産霊尊]]ー'''天活玉命'''ー天押立命ー陶津耳命ー'''玉依彦命'''ー生玉兄日子命（賀茂氏祖）」となっている。また『難波田使首系図』には「'''天活玉命'''（伊久魂命）ー天押立命（'''神櫛玉命'''）ー陶津耳命ー'''玉依彦命'''」と続いており、これに従えば「天神玉命（天活玉命、伊久魂命）ー天櫛玉命（神櫛玉命、天押立命）ー加茂建角身命（八咫烏、陶津耳命）ー鴨建玉依彦命（玉依彦命）ー五十手美命（生玉兄日子命）」となる<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族の研究⑬ 天皇氏族 天孫族の来た道』青垣出版、2018年。 </ref>。
娘には[[Emperor Jimmu|神武天皇]]の妻である[[Himetataraisuzu-hime|比売多々良伊須気余理比売]]の母・勢夜陀多良比売（活玉依毘売、玉櫛媛、玉依姫命、建玉依姫命）がいる。言い換えれば、加茂建角身命は神武天皇の岳母の父にあたり、加茂建角身命を八咫烏と同一神とすると、世代関係が大きく矛盾してしまうことになるため、実際に神武天皇の道案内をしたのは加茂建角身命の孫である生玉兄日子命と考えられている<ref>宝賀寿男『古代氏族の研究⑬ 天皇氏族 天孫族の来た道』青垣出版、2018年。 </ref>。
なお、八咫烏は『[[Kojiki|古事記]]』や『[[Nihon Shoki|日本書紀]]』に登場するが、『日本書紀』では、同じ神武東征の場面で、金鵄（金色の[[Black kite|トビ]]）が長髄彦との戦いで神武天皇を助けたともされており、天日鷲神の別名である天加奈止美命（あめのかなとみ）の名称が金鵄（かなとび）に通じることから、天日鷲神、賀茂建角身命と同一視する説を[[Hirata Atsutane|平田篤胤]]などが唱えている<ref>宝賀寿男「神武天皇、実在の可能性」『「神武天皇」伝承の真実を検証する⑦』、2017年。 </ref>。
== 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}{{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 ==
* [[Shimogamo Shrine|賀茂御祖神社]] * [[List of Japanese deities|日本の神の一覧]] [[Category:Japanese gods]]

'''Kamo Kenkakumikami no Mikoto''' is a [[Kami|God]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. Kamotaketsunomikoto''', also known as '''Kamotaketsunomikoto''',<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>.
== Overview == He is also known by the names '''Yatagarasu''', '''Yatagarasu Kamo Takekazumi no Mikoto''', '''Mishima no Mizokui''', '''Mishima Mizokui''', '''Mishima Mizokusaikomi no Kami'', '''Suetsumimi no Mikoto''', '''Suetsumimi''' and '''Amahigatakushihigata Takechinutsumi'''.
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[Yamashiro Province]], the [[Kamo Prefecture]] and the Katsuragi Province, and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsengumi Roku]], Kamo Kenkakumimikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi|Kamitonomikoto]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto]], under the command of [[Takamimusubi|Takagi-kami]] and [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamo Kenkakumimikoto had two sons, Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto and Taketamayorihime no-mikoto. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[[Himetataraisuzu-haru Himetataraisuzu-hime|Hime Totobori Isuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == The genealogy of the Kamo clan is "[[Kamimusubi|Kami-no-Sanrei-tsun-no-Mikoto]]-Tenjin-tama-no-Mikoto-'''[[Futodama|Tenkushitama-no-Mikoto]]''''-Kamo-tatemikazumi-no-Mikoto-''''Kamo-tatemuyorihiko-no-Mikoto''''-Gojutemi-no-Mikoto (ancestor of the Kamo clan). Takamimusubi]]-'''Amakatatama-no-mikoto'', ''Amaoshitate-no-mikoto'', ''Tohtsumimi-no-mikoto'', ''Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto'', and ''Ikutama-ni-no-mikoto'' (ancestor of the Kamo clan). The Naniwada Shusshusu genealogical chart continues, "'''Amakatadama-no-mikoto'' - Amashitadama-no-mikoto (''Kamikushi-tamamikoto'') - To-tsumimi-no-mikoto - '''Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto'''", which means, "Tenjin-tamamikoto (Amakatadama-no-mikoto, Ikutama-no-mikoto) - Amakushi-tamamikoto (Kamikushi-tamikoto, Amashitadama-no-mikoto) - Kamo-tenkakumikami-no-mikoto (Yatagarasu, To-tsumimi-no-mikoto) - Kamo-taketama-yorihiko-no-mikoto (Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto)". Yorihiko-no-mikoto (Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto) - Gojutemi-no-mikoto (Ikutama-nihiko-no-mikoto)."<ref>[[Hisao Hoga]], Studies on Ancient Clans ⑬: The Emperor's Clan, The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>.
His daughters include [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetaraisuzu-yori-hime]]'s mother, Seiyatara-hime (活玉依毘売, Tamagushi-hime, Tamayori-hime no Mikoto, Tatamayori-hime no Mikoto). In other words, Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>.
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi Ame-no-kanatomi-no-mikoto, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] and others<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>.
== 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}{{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 ==
* [[Shimogamo Shrine|賀茂御祖神社]] * [[List of Japanese deities|日本の神の一覧]] [[Category:Japanese gods]]

'''Kamo Kenkakumikami no Mikoto''' is a [[Kami|God]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. Kamotaketsunomikoto''', also known as '''Kamotaketsunomikoto''',<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>.
== Overview == He is also known by the names '''Yatagarasu''', '''Yatagarasu Kamo Takekazumi no Mikoto''', '''Mishima no Mizokui''', '''Mishima Mizokui''', '''Mishima Mizokusaikomi no Kami'', '''Suetsumimi no Mikoto''', '''Suetsumimi''' and '''Amahigatakushihigata Takechinutsumi'''.
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[Yamashiro Province]], the [[Kamo Prefecture]] and the Katsuragi Province, and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsengumi Roku]], Kamo Kenkakumimikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi|Kamitonomikoto]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto]], under the command of [[Takamimusubi|Takagi-kami]] and [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamo Kenkakumimikoto had two sons, Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto and Taketamayorihime no-mikoto. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[[Himetataraisuzu-haru Himetataraisuzu-hime|Hime Totobori Isuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime|Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == The genealogy of the Kamo clan is "[[Kamimusubi|Kami-no-Sanrei-tsun-no-Mikoto]]-Tenjin-tama-no-Mikoto-'''[[Futodama|Tenkushitama-no-Mikoto]]''''-Kamo-tatemikazumi-no-Mikoto-''''Kamo-tatemuyorihiko-no-Mikoto''''-Gojutemi-no-Mikoto (ancestor of the Kamo clan). Takamimusubi]]-'''Amakatatama-no-mikoto'', ''Amaoshitate-no-mikoto'', ''Tohtsumimi-no-mikoto'', ''Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto'', and ''Ikutama-ni-no-mikoto'' (ancestor of the Kamo clan). The Naniwada Shusshusu genealogical chart continues, "'''Amakatadama-no-mikoto'' - Amashitadama-no-mikoto (''Kamikushi-tamamikoto'') - To-tsumimi-no-mikoto - '''Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto'''", which means, "Tenjin-tamamikoto (Amakatadama-no-mikoto, Ikutama-no-mikoto) - Amakushi-tamamikoto (Kamikushi-tamikoto, Amashitadama-no-mikoto) - Kamo-tenkakumikami-no-mikoto (Yatagarasu, To-tsumimi-no-mikoto) - Kamo-taketama-yorihiko-no-mikoto (Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto)". Yorihiko-no-mikoto (Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto) - Gojutemi-no-mikoto (Ikutama-nihiko-no-mikoto)."<ref>[[Hisao Hoga]], Studies on Ancient Clans ⑬: The Emperor's Clan, The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>.
His daughters include [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetaraisuzu-yori-hime]]'s mother, Seiyatara-hime (活玉依毘売, Tamagushi-hime, Tamayori-hime no Mikoto, Tatamayori-hime no Mikoto). In other words, Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>.
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi Ame-no-kanatomi-no-mikoto, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] and others<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>.
== 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}{{Reflist}}
== See Also ==
* [[Shimogamo Shrine]] * [[List of Japanese deities]] [[Category:Japanese gods]]

{{Short description|Japanese mythical god}} {{refimprove|date=November 2021}}
'''Kamo Kenkakumikami no Mikoto''' is a [[Kami|God]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. Kamotaketsunomikoto''', also known as '''Kamotaketsunomikoto''',<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>.
== Overview == He is also known by the names '''Yatagarasu''', '''Yatagarasu Kamo Takekazumi no Mikoto''', '''Mishima no Mizokui''', '''Mishima Mizokui''', '''Mishima Mizokusaikomi no Kami'', '''Suetsumimi no Mikoto''', '''Suetsumimi''' and '''Amahigatakushihigata Takechinutsumi'''.
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[Yamashiro Province]], the [[Kamo Prefecture]] and the Katsuragi Province, and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsengumi Roku]], Kamo Kenkakumimikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi|Kamitonomikoto]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto]], under the command of [[Takamimusubi|Takagi-kami]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamo Kenkakumimikoto had two sons, Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto and Taketamayorihime no-mikoto. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[[Himetataraisuzu-haru Himetataraisuzu-hime|Hime Totobori Isuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == The genealogy of the Kamo clan is "[[Kamimusubi|Kami-no-Sanrei-tsun-no-Mikoto]]-Tenjin-tama-no-Mikoto-'''[[Futodama|Tenkushitama-no-Mikoto]]''''-Kamo-tatemikazumi-no-Mikoto-''''Kamo-tatemuyorihiko-no-Mikoto''''-Gojutemi-no-Mikoto (ancestor of the Kamo clan). Takamimusubi]]-'''Amakatatama-no-mikoto'', ''Amaoshitate-no-mikoto'', ''Tohtsumimi-no-mikoto'', ''Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto'', and ''Ikutama-ni-no-mikoto'' (ancestor of the Kamo clan). The Naniwada Shusshusu genealogical chart continues, "'''Amakatadama-no-mikoto'' - Amashitadama-no-mikoto (''Kamikushi-tamamikoto'') - To-tsumimi-no-mikoto - '''Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto'''", which means, "Tenjin-tamamikoto (Amakatadama-no-mikoto, Ikutama-no-mikoto) - Amakushi-tamamikoto (Kamikushi-tamikoto, Amashitadama-no-mikoto) - Kamo-tenkakumikami-no-mikoto (Yatagarasu, To-tsumimi-no-mikoto) - Kamo-taketama-yorihiko-no-mikoto (Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto)". Yorihiko-no-mikoto (Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto) - Gojutemi-no-mikoto (Ikutama-nihiko-no-mikoto)."<ref>[[Hisao Hoga]], Studies on Ancient Clans ⑬: The Emperor's Clan, The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>.
His daughters include [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetaraisuzu-yori-hime]]'s mother, Seiyatara-hime (活玉依毘売, Tamagushi-hime, Tamayori-hime no Mikoto, Tatamayori-hime no Mikoto). In other words, Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>.
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi Ame-no-kanatomi-no-mikoto, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

The Naniwada Shusshusu genealogical chart continues, "'''Amakatadama-no-mikoto'' - Amashitadama-no-mikoto (''Kamikushi-tamamikoto'') - To-tsumimi-no-mikoto - '''Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto'''", which means, "Tenjin-tamamikoto (Amakatadama-no-mikoto, Ikutama-no-mikoto) - Amakushi-tamamikoto (Kamikushi-tamikoto, Amashitadama-no-mikoto) - Kamo-tenkakumikami-no-mikoto (Yatagarasu, To-tsumimi-no-mikoto) - Kamo-taketama-yorihiko-no-mikoto (Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto)". Yorihiko-no-mikoto (Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto) - Gojutemi-no-mikoto (Ikutama-nihiko-no-mikoto)."<ref>[[Hisao Hoga]], Studies on Ancient Clans ⑬: The Emperor's Clan, The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
His daughters include [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, {{Interlanguage link|Himetataraisuzu-hime|lt=|ja|ヒメタタライスズヒメ}}'s mother, Seiyatara-hime (活玉依毘売, Tamagushi-hime, Tamayori-hime no Mikoto, Tatamayori-hime no Mikoto). In other words, Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi Ame-no-kanatomi-no-mikoto, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythical god}} {{refimprove|date=November 2021}}
'''Kamo Kenkakumikami no Mikoto''' is a [[Kami|God]] in [[Japanese mythology]], also known as '''Kamotaketsunomikoto'''.<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>
== Overview == He is also known by the names '''[[Yatagarasu]]''', '''Yatagarasu Kamo Takekazumi no Mikoto''', '''Mishima no Mizokui''', '''Mishima Mizokui''', '''Mishima Mizokusaikomi no Kami''', '''Suetsumimi no Mikoto''', '''Suetsumimi''' and '''Amahigatakushihigata Takechinutsumi'''.
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[Yamashiro Province]], the [[Kamo Prefecture]] and the Katsuragi Province, and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsengumi Roku]], Kamo Kenkakumimikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi|Kamitonomikoto]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto]], under the command of [[Takamimusubi|Takagi-kami]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamo Kenkakumimikoto had two sons, Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto and Taketamayorihime no-mikoto. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, {{Interlanguage link|Himetataraisuzu-hime|lt=|ja|ヒメタタライスズヒメ}} and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == The genealogy of the Kamo clan is "[[Kamimusubi|Kami-no-Sanrei-tsun-no-Mikoto]]-Tenjin-tama-no-Mikoto-'''[[Futodama|Tenkushitama-no-Mikoto]]'''-Kamo-tatemikazumi-no-Mikoto-'''Kamo-tatemuyorihiko-no-Mikoto'''-Gojutemi-no-Mikoto (ancestor of the Kamo clan). Takamimusubi]]-''Amakatatama-no-mikoto'', ''Amaoshitate-no-mikoto'', ''Tohtsumimi-no-mikoto'', ''Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto'', and ''Ikutama-ni-no-mikoto'' (ancestor of the Kamo clan). The Naniwada Shusshusu genealogical chart continues, "'''Amakatadama-no-mikoto'' - Amashitadama-no-mikoto (''Kamikushi-tamamikoto'') - To-tsumimi-no-mikoto - '''Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto'''", which means, "Tenjin-tamamikoto (Amakatadama-no-mikoto, Ikutama-no-mikoto) - Amakushi-tamamikoto (Kamikushi-tamikoto, Amashitadama-no-mikoto) - Kamo-tenkakumikami-no-mikoto (Yatagarasu, To-tsumimi-no-mikoto) - Kamo-taketama-yorihiko-no-mikoto (Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto)". Yorihiko-no-mikoto (Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto) - Gojutemi-no-mikoto (Ikutama-nihiko-no-mikoto)."<ref>[[Hisao Hoga]], Studies on Ancient Clans ⑬: The Emperor's Clan, The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
His daughters include [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, {{Interlanguage link|Himetataraisuzu-hime|lt=|ja|ヒメタタライスズヒメ}}'s mother, Seiyatara-hime (活玉依毘売, Tamagushi-hime, Tamayori-hime no Mikoto, Tatamayori-hime no Mikoto). In other words, Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi Ame-no-kanatomi-no-mikoto, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == The genealogy of the Kamo clan is "[[Kamimusubi|Kami-no-Sanrei-tsun-no-Mikoto]]-Tenjin-tama-no-Mikoto-'''[[Futodama|Tenkushitama-no-Mikoto]]'''-Kamo-tatemikazumi-no-Mikoto-'''Kamo-tatemuyorihiko-no-Mikoto'''-Gojutemi-no-Mikoto (ancestor of the Kamo clan). Takamimusubi]]-''Amakatatama-no-mikoto'', ''Amaoshitate-no-mikoto'', ''Tohtsumimi-no-mikoto'', ''Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto'', and ''Ikutama-ni-no-mikoto'' (ancestor of the Kamo clan). The Naniwada Shusshusu genealogical chart continues, "'''Amakatadama-no-mikoto'' - Amashitadama-no-mikoto (''Kamikushi-tamamikoto'') - To-tsumimi-no-mikoto - '''Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto'''", which means, "Tenjin-tamamikoto (Amakatadama-no-mikoto, Ikutama-no-mikoto) - Amakushi-tamamikoto (Kamikushi-tamikoto, Amashitadama-no-mikoto) - Kamo-tenkakumikami-no-mikoto (Yatagarasu, To-tsumimi-no-mikoto) - Kamo-taketama-yorihiko-no-mikoto (Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto)". Yorihiko-no-mikoto (Tamayorihiko-no-mikoto) - Gojutemi-no-mikoto (Ikutama-nihiko-no-mikoto)."<ref>[[Hisao Hoga]], Studies on Ancient Clans ⑬: The Emperor's Clan, The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
His daughters include [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]'s mother, Seiyatara-hime (活玉依毘売, Tamagushi-hime, Tamayori-hime no Mikoto, Tatamayori-hime no Mikoto). In other words, Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi Ame-no-kanatomi-no-mikoto, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]'s mother, Seiyatara-hime (活玉依毘売, Tamagushi-hime, Tamayori-hime no Mikoto, Tatamayori-hime no Mikoto). In other words, Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamo no Kenkakumimikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi Ame-no-kanatomi-no-mikoto, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi [[Ame-no-Kanatomi|Ame-no-kanatomi]]-no-mikoto, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythical god}} {{refimprove|date=November 2021}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Tamakushi-hime]] | father = [[Futodama]] | greek_equivalent = [[Helios]] }} '''Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto''' is a [[Kami|God]] in [[Japanese mythology]].<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>
== Overview == He is also known by the name '''[[Yatagarasu]].'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&pg=PA320&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&hl=en |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref>
Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.
'''Kamotaketsunumi'''
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[Yamashiro Province]], and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto and Taketamayorihime no-mikoto. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi [[Ame-no-Kanatomi|Ame-no-kanatomi]]-no-mikoto, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythical god}} {{refimprove|date=November 2021}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = [[Tamakushi-hime]] [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]] | father = [[Futodama]] | greek_equivalent = [[Helios]] }} '''Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto''' (三嶋溝抗命) is a [[Kami|God]] in [[Japanese mythology]].<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>
== Overview == He is also known by the name '''[[Yatagarasu]].'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref>
Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.
'''Kamotaketsunumi'''
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[Yamashiro Province]], and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Ame-no-Kanatomi|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>
==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also ==
* [[Shimogamo Shrine]] * [[List of Japanese deities]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
[[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Amatsukami]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] {{Kamo Faith}}

revision.comment: capitalized "God" (conventionally used specifically to refer to the Judeo-Christian god) in first sentence changed to lower-case "god"

== Overview == He is also known by the name '''[[Yatagarasu]].'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref>
Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.
'''Kamotaketsunumi'''
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[Yamashiro Province]], and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Ame-no-Kanatomi|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythical god}} {{refimprove|date=November 2021}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Yatagarasu/Kamotaketsunumi | deity_of = Personification of Sun | children = [[Tamakushi-hime]] [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]] | father = [[Futodama]] | greek_equivalent = [[Helios]] }} '''Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto''' (三嶋溝抗命) is a [[Kami|god]] in [[Japanese mythology]].<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>
== Overview == He is also known by the name '''[[Yatagarasu]].'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref>
Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.
'''Kamotaketsunumi'''
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[Yamashiro Province]], and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Ame-no-Kanatomi|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Ame-no-Kanatomi|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

According to [[Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.
'''Kamotaketsunumi'''
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[Yamashiro Province]], and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Ame-no-Kanatomi|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>
==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also ==
* [[Shimogamo Shrine]] * [[List of Japanese deities]]

In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythical god}} {{refimprove|date=November 2021}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Yatagarasu/Kamotaketsunumi | deity_of = Personification of Sun | children = [[Tamakushi-hime]] [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]] | father = [[Futodama]] | greek_equivalent = [[Helios]] }} '''Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto''' (三嶋溝抗命) is a [[Kami|god]] in [[Japanese mythology]].<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>
== Overview == He is also known by the name '''[[Yatagarasu]].'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref>
Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.
'''Kamotaketsunumi'''
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[Yamashiro Province]], and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>
==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also ==
* [[Shimogamo Shrine]] * [[List of Japanese deities]]

Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.
'''Kamotaketsunumi'''

== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythical god}} {{refimprove|date=November 2021}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Yatagarasu/Kamotaketsunumi | deity_of = Personification of Sun | children = [[Tamakushi-hime]] [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]] | father = [[Futodama]] | greek_equivalent = [[Helios]] }} '''Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto''' (三嶋溝抗命) is a [[Kami|god]] in [[Japanese mythology]].<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>
== Overview == He is also known by the name '''[[Yatagarasu]].'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref>
Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.
'''Kamotaketsunumi'''
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[Yamashiro Province]], and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythical god}} {{refimprove|date=November 2021}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Yatagarasu/Kamotaketsunumi | deity_of = Personification of Sun | children = [[Tamakushi-hime]] [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]] | father = [[Futodama]] | greek_equivalent = [[Helios]] }} '''Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto''' (三嶋溝抗命) is a [[Kami|god]] in [[Japanese mythology]].<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>
== Overview == He is also known by the name '''[[Yatagarasu]].'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref>
Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.
'''Kamotaketsunumi'''
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[Yamashiro Province]], and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

'''Kamotaketsunumi'''
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[Yamashiro Province]], and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythical god}} {{refimprove|date=November 2021}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Yatagarasu/Kamotaketsunumi | deity_of = Personification of Sun | children = [[Tamakushi-hime]] [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]] | father = [[Futodama]] | greek_equivalent = [[Helios]] }} '''Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto''' (三嶋溝抗命) is a [[Kami|god]] in [[Japanese mythology]].<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>
== Overview == He is also known by the name '''[[Yatagarasu]].'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref>
Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.
'''Kamotaketsunumi'''
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[Yamashiro Province]], and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythical god}} {{refimprove|date=November 2021}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Yatagarasu/Kamotaketsunumi | deity_of = Personification of Sun | children = [[Tamakushi-hime]] [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]] | father = [[Futodama]] | greek_equivalent = [[Helios]] }} '''Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto''' (三嶋溝抗命) is a [[Kami|god]] in [[Japanese mythology]].<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>
== Overview == He is also known by the name '''[[Yatagarasu]].'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref>
Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.
'''Kamotaketsunumi'''
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[Yamashiro Province]], and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>

According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese mythical god}} {{refimprove|date=November 2021}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Yatagarasu/Kamotaketsunumi | deity_of = Personification of Sun | children = [[Tamakushi-hime]] [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]] | father = [[Futodama]] | greek_equivalent = [[Helios]] }} '''Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto''' (三嶋溝抗命) is a [[Kami|god]] in [[Japanese mythology]].<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>
== Overview == He is also known by the name '''[[Yatagarasu]].'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref>
Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.
'''Kamotaketsunumi'''
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[Yamashiro Province]], and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>
==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also ==
* [[Shimogamo Shrine]] * [[List of Japanese deities]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
{{Kamo Faith}}
