Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.
'''Kamotaketsunumi'''
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[Yamashiro Province]], and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

== Overview == He is also known by the name '''[[Yatagarasu]].'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref>
Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.

{{Short description|Japanese mythical god}} {{refimprove|date=November 2021}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Yatagarasu/Kamotaketsunumi | deity_of = Personification of Sun | children = [[Tamakushi-hime]] [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]] | father = [[Futodama]] | greek_equivalent = [[Helios]] }} '''Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto''' (三嶋溝抗命) is a [[Kami|god]] in [[Japanese mythology]].<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>
== Overview == He is also known by the name '''[[Yatagarasu]].'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref>
Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.
'''Kamotaketsunumi'''
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[Yamashiro Province]], and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[:en:Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>
==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also ==
* [[Shimogamo Shrine]] * [[List of Japanese deities]]
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Kamo Faith}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Amatsukami]] [[Category:Kamo clan]]
}}

In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[:en:Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[:en:Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[:en:Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

== Overview == He is also known by the name '''[[:en:Yatagarasu|Yatagarasu]].'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref>
Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.
'''Kamotaketsunumi'''
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[:en:Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

'''Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto''' (三嶋溝抗命) is a [[Kami|god]] in [[Japanese mythology]].<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>

== Overview ==
He is also known by the name '''[[:en:Yatagarasu|Yatagarasu]].'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref> 

Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity. 

'''Kamotaketsunumi''' 

He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).

According to [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.

According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).

Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].

== Genealogy ==
His father is [[Futodama]]

His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>

Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[:en:Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

==Family tree==
{{Eight generations of Izumo}}

'''Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto''' (三嶋溝抗命) is a [[Kami|god]] in [[Japanese mythology]].<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>

== Overview ==
He is also known by the name '''[[:en:Yatagarasu|Yatagarasu]].'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref> 

Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity. 

'''Kamotaketsunumi''' 

He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).

According to [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.

According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).

Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].

== Genealogy ==
His father is [[Futodama]]

His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>

Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[:en:Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

==Family tree==
{{Eight generations of Izumo}}

'''Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto''' (三嶋溝抗命) is a [[Kami|god]] in [[Japanese mythology]].<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>

== Overview ==
He is also known by the name '''[[:en:Yatagarasu|Yatagarasu]].'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref> 

Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity. 

'''Kamotaketsunumi''' 

He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).

According to [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.

According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).

Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].

== Genealogy ==
His father is [[Futodama]]

His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>

Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[:en:Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

==Family tree==

[[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]<!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:賀茂建角身命]] [[zh:賀茂建角身命]] {{wikidata link|Q11634939}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]][[ja:賀茂建角身命]][[zh:賀茂建角身命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]][[ja:賀茂建角身命]][[zh:賀茂建角身命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]][[ja:賀茂建角身命]][[zh:賀茂建角身命]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634939]] Wikidata name: Kamo Taketsunomi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634939]] Wikidata name: Kamo Taketsunomi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese mythical god}} {{refimprove|date=November 2021}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Yatagarasu/Kamotaketsunumi | deity_of = Personification of Sun | children = [[Tamakushi-hime]] [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]] | father = [[Futodama]] | greek_equivalent = [[:en:Helios|Helios]] | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | parents = {{ill|Futodama|WD=Q11059539}} | offspring = {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|WD=Q11572681}}; {{ill|Tamayorihiko no Mikoto|WD=Q106380234}} | relatives = {{ill|Kamo no Chōmei|WD=Q291329}}; {{ill|Kamo no Mabuchi|WD=Q1343397}}; {{ill|Q97184246|WD=Q97184246}} | equivalent5_type = Japanese | equivalent5 = {{ill|Yatagarasu|WD=Q745463}} }} '''Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto''' (三嶋溝抗命) is a [[Kami|god]] in [[Japanese mythology]].<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>
== Overview == He is also known by the name '''[[:en:Yatagarasu|Yatagarasu]].'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref>
Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.
'''Kamotaketsunumi'''
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[:en:Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>
==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also ==

Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[:en:Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]][[ja:賀茂建角身命]][[zh:賀茂建角身命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]][[ja:賀茂建角身命]][[zh:賀茂建角身命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]][[ja:賀茂建角身命]][[zh:賀茂建角身命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]][[ja:賀茂建角身命]][[zh:賀茂建角身命]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634939]] Wikidata name: Kamo Taketsunomi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634939]] Wikidata name: Kamo Taketsunomi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese mythical god}} {{refimprove|date=November 2021}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Yatagarasu/Kamotaketsunumi | deity_of = Personification of Sun | children = [[Tamakushi-hime]] [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]] | father = [[Futodama]] | greek_equivalent = [[:en:Helios|Helios]] | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | parents = {{ill|Futodama|WD=Q11059539}} | offspring = {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|WD=Q11572681}}; {{ill|Tamayorihiko no Mikoto|WD=Q106380234}} | relatives = {{ill|Kamo no Chōmei|WD=Q291329}}; {{ill|Kamo no Mabuchi|WD=Q1343397}}; {{ill|Q97184246|WD=Q97184246}} | equivalent5_type = Japanese | equivalent5 = {{ill|Yatagarasu|WD=Q745463}} }} '''Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto''' (三嶋溝抗命) is a [[Kami|god]] in [[Japanese mythology]].<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>
== Overview == He is also known by the name '''[[:en:Yatagarasu|Yatagarasu]].'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref>
Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.
'''Kamotaketsunumi'''
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]], where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden [[:en:Black kite|Tobi]]) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato}}, where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}} and contributed to his victory.

Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden {{ill|Black kite|en|Black kite|WD=Q80362|lt=Tobi}}) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>
==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also ==

Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.
'''Kamotaketsunumi'''
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province}}, and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).

Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato}}, where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}} and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).

Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>

{{Short description|Japanese mythical god}} {{refimprove|date=November 2021}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Yatagarasu/Kamotaketsunumi | deity_of = Personification of Sun | children = [[Tamakushi-hime]] [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]] | father = [[Futodama]] | greek_equivalent = {{ill|Helios|en|Helios|WD=Q134270|lt=Helios}} | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | parents = {{ill|Futodama|WD=Q11059539}} | offspring = {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|WD=Q11572681}}; {{ill|Tamayorihiko no Mikoto|WD=Q106380234}} | relatives = {{ill|Kamo no Chōmei|WD=Q291329}}; {{ill|Kamo no Mabuchi|WD=Q1343397}}; {{ill|Q97184246|WD=Q97184246}} | equivalent5_type = Japanese | equivalent5 = {{ill|Yatagarasu|WD=Q745463}} }} '''Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto''' (三嶋溝抗命) is a [[Kami|god]] in [[Japanese mythology]].<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>
== Overview == He is also known by the name '''{{ill|Yatagarasu|en|Yatagarasu|WD=Q745463|lt=Yatagarasu}}.'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref>
Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.
'''Kamotaketsunumi'''
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province}}, and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato}}, where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}} and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>
Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden {{ill|Black kite|en|Black kite|WD=Q80362|lt=Tobi}}) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>
==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also ==
* [[Shimogamo Shrine]] * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities}}

{{Short description|Japanese mythical god}} {{refimprove|date=November 2021}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Yatagarasu/Kamotaketsunumi | deity_of = Personification of Sun | children = [[Tamakushi-hime]] [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]] | father = [[Futodama]] | greek_equivalent = {{ill|Helios|en|Helios|WD=Q134270|lt=Helios|1=Helios}} | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}} | parents = {{ill|Futodama|WD=Q11059539|1=Futodama}} | offspring = {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|WD=Q11572681|1=Tamakushi-hime}}; {{ill|Tamayorihiko no Mikoto|WD=Q106380234|1=Tamayorihiko no Mikoto}} | relatives = {{ill|Kamo no Chōmei|WD=Q291329|1=Kamo no Chōmei}}; {{ill|Kamo no Mabuchi|WD=Q1343397|1=Kamo no Mabuchi}}; {{ill|Q97184246|WD=Q97184246|1=Ame-no-Ikutama}} | equivalent5_type = Japanese | equivalent5 = {{ill|Yatagarasu|WD=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}} }} '''Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto''' (三嶋溝抗命) is a [[Kami|god]] in [[Japanese mythology]].<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>
== Overview == He is also known by the name '''{{ill|Yatagarasu|en|Yatagarasu|WD=Q745463|lt=Yatagarasu|1=Yatagarasu}}.'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref>
Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity.
'''Kamotaketsunumi'''
He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}, and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).
According to {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}, where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} and contributed to his victory.
According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).
Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].
== Genealogy == His father is [[Futodama]]
His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>

'''Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto''' (三嶋溝抗命) is a [[Kami|god]] in [[Japanese mythology]].<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>

== Overview ==
He is also known by the name '''{{ill|Yatagarasu|en|Yatagarasu|WD=Q745463|lt=Yatagarasu|1=Yatagarasu}}.'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref> 

Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity. 

'''Kamotaketsunumi''' 

He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}, and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).

According to {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}, where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} and contributed to his victory.

According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).

Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].

== Genealogy ==
His father is [[Futodama]]

His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>

Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden {{ill|Black kite|en|Black kite|WD=Q80362|lt=Tobi|1=Black kite}}) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>

'''Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto''' (三嶋溝抗命) is a [[Kami|god]] in [[Japanese mythology]].<ref>Britannica International Encyclopedia - Electronic Subdivision Edition. </ref>

== Overview ==
He is also known by the name '''{{ill|Yatagarasu|en|Yatagarasu|qid=Q745463|lt=Yatagarasu|1=Yatagarasu}}.'''<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ&dq=yatagarasu+kamo+clan&pg=PA320 |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}</ref> 

Kamotakesunumi is his name; '-no-Mikoto' is an honorific, denoting divinity. 

'''Kamotaketsunumi''' 

He is the founder of the [[Kamo clan]] of {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|qid=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}, and is known as the deity of the [[Shimogamo Shrine]] (Shimogamo Shrine).

According to {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is the grandson of [[Kamimusubi]]. Kamo Kenkakumikuninomikoto, under the command of [[Takamimusubi]] and [[Amaterasu]], descended from the sky to the peak of Tsune in Hyuga and reached Mount Katsuragi in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato Province}}, where he incarnated as Yatagarasu and led {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} and contributed to his victory.

According to the Yamashiro-kuni Fudoki (an anecdote), he came from Katsuragiyama in Yamato to Kamo in Okada in Yamashiro (where the Okada Kamo Shrine is located) and settled at the confluence of the Katsuno River (Koya River) and the Kamo River (Kamo River) (where the Shimogamo Shrine is located).

Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto had two sons, [[Taketamayorihiko-no-mikoto]] and [[Taketamayorihime no-mikoto]]. Taketamayorihikonomikoto later became the Lord of Kamo Prefecture. Taketamayorihime is said to have conceived and given birth to Kamo Bessarai-no-mikoto (the deity of [[Kamigamo Shrine]]) while keeping [[Yakusanoikazuchi|Honokazuchi-no-kami]], who was incarnated as a Tanuria arrow, near her floor (Yamashiro However, in the [[Kojiki]], the [[Nihon Shoki]], and the [[Kujiki|Sekaiyo Koshihonki]], she is said to have become the wife of [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] or [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi]], and to have given birth to Kushimikata-no-mikoto, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] and [[Isuzuyori-hime]].

== Genealogy ==
His father is [[Futodama]]

His daughters include [[Tamakushi-hime]] the mother of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}'s wife, [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. In other words, Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto was the father of Emperor Jimmu's mother, and if Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto is considered to be the same god as Yatagarasu, the generation relationship would be very inconsistent. <ref>The Emperor's Clans: The Way of the Descendants, Aogaki Publishing, 2018. </ref>

Yatagarasu appears in [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]], and in [[Nihon Shoki]], it is also said that Kin Kite (golden {{ill|Black kite|en|Black kite|qid=Q80362|lt=Tobi|1=Black kite}}) helped Emperor Jinmu in his battle with Naganomushihiko in the same scene of Jinmu's eastern expedition, which is another name for the god Amanohiwashi {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|lt=Ame-no-Kanatomi-no-mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=}}, whose name is related to kinshi (golden kite), and therefore is considered to be the same as Ame-no-hikawashi-no-mikoto and Kamo-kenkakumikami-no-mikoto by [[Hirata Atsutane]] and others.<ref>Tsuo Hoga, "Emperor Jinmu, the possibility of his existence", in "Verifying the truth of the "Emperor Jinmu" tradition", 7. 2017. </ref>
